recipes.title: BBQ Bacon Nachos
recipes.description: This recipe has been created using FareShare FLEX bacon in BBQ stock which is ingredient-packed, flavourful, and can be used in a range of everyday meals.
ingredients_raw: Sour Cream, Cherry Tomatos and Peppers to serve
ingredient_names: sour cream, cherry tomatos and peppers to serve
method: Open can and empty contents into a saucepan, heat until you can start to shred the meat.
Add garlic, paprika, herbs and pinch of salt and pepper, then stir and cook until hot.
Mix your soured cream and chopped chives and set aside.
Once cooked, serve over the nachos, top with cheese, salads and sour cream

recipes.title: BBQ Bacon Loaded Skins
recipes.description: This recipe has been created using FareShare FLEX bacon in BBQ stock which is ingredient-packed, flavourful, and can be used in a range of everyday meals.
method: Open can and empty contents into a saucepan, heat until you can start to shred the meat. Once hot, taste and season as required.
Cut your jacket potatoes in half and scoop the middles into a bowl, add your cheese and BBQ Bacon, mix together until combined. Place this mixture back into your potato skins.
Place in airfryer or on a tray in the oven and cook for 10/15 mins at 180 until golden brown.
Once cooked, serve with salad and maybe some soured cream with chives.

recipes.title: BBQ Bacon Breakfast Hash
recipes.description: This recipe has been created using FareShare FLEX bacon in BBQ stock which is ingredient-packed, flavourful, and can be used in a range of everyday meals.
ingredients_raw: 300g Cooked Potatoes (from a can or fresh)
method: Open can and empty contents into a saucepan, heat until you can start to shred the meat.
Add garlic powder, smoked paprika and mixed herbs and slowly simmer. Once hot, taste and add seasoning if required.
Half or quarter your cooked potatoes, place in an airfryer with your chopped onions and a few sprays of cooking oil. Cook on 170 for 10 minutes.
While cooking your potatoes, fry your eggs using a little oil.
Place potatoes and onions on a plate, top with shredded Bacon in BBQ Stock and your eggs. Sprinkle with fresh or dried herbs and maybe a bit of mayonnaise.

recipes.title: Summer Vegetable Traybake
recipes.description: This delicious recipe was written by Joanne Iceton at Little Chefs , Big Chefs. Enjoy!
ingredients_raw: rapeseed or olive oil for drizzling or use whatever oil you have
2 courgettes cut into chunks (I used 5 baby courgettes here)
4 spring onions sliced into 2cm lengths (or use 1 onion cut into wedges)
1-2 garlic cloves sliced (or 1-2 tsp. garlic paste)
400 g tomatoes roughly chopped or whole cherry tomatoes (or 1 x 400g tin chopped tomatoes)
Handful fresh basil leaves torn into pieces or 2 tbsp. pesto
4 – 5 thick slices stale bread I used cornbread Stale bread is drier and absorbs more of the delicious juices
ingredient_names: rapeseed or olive oil for drizzling or use whatever oil you have
tomatoes roughly chopped or whole cherry tomatoes
handful fresh basil leaves torn into pieces or 2 tbsp. pesto
thick slices stale bread i used cornbread stale bread is drier and absorbs more of the delicious juices
method:
Oven 180c. 
Prepare the vegetables. Add the peppers, courgettes, onions, garlic and chilli powder, if using, in an ovenproof baking tray. Drizzle over some oil, toss together and season. Bake for 15 mins until the veg starts to char and soften.
Toss in the tomatoes, vinegar and half the basil leaves or pesto, mix well and return to the oven for 5 - 10 mins whilst you cut the bread into cubes and grate the cheese.

Once the tomatoes start to char, scatter the bread cubes over the top. Press down using the back of a spoon or your hand if you are careful so some of the bread is submerged in the juices. Sprinkle over the cheese then return to the oven until the bread toasts and the cheese melts (5 - 10 mins), Scatter the remaining basil leaves or pesto over to serve.

recipes.title: Parsnip Muffins with Cream Cheese Frosting
recipes.description: This delicious recipe was written by Lisa Musson at FareShare Merseyside. Enjoy!
method: Set oven at 170c.
Add all muffin ingredients to a bowl and mix thoroughly. If the batter is too thick add a small amount of water a tsp at a time.
Spoon into muffin cases in a muffin tray - this will make 12 large muffins or 24 small muffins.
Bake for 15 minutes, until risen and golden.
Whilst baking make the frosting.
Whisk the butter until there are no lumps.
Add in the cream cheese and icing sugar and whisk until light and fluffy  *Top Tip! It should change from a pale yellow colour to white
Once cakes are cooled, place frosting in the bag and pipe as desired
Eat!
recipes.cook_time: Bake for 15 minutes, until risen and golden.
